summary: Frontend-only e2e verification of iteration 2 (dark theme + finance KPIs + Request Budget). Every review-request item verified: dark bg #08080C, Ethara.AI sidebar with 9 nav items (Budget Consolidation removed), 'Financial Command Center' title, new 8-KPI grid (kpi-approved-budget/actual-spend/eac/cpi/burn-rate/runway/ai-ratio/pending-approvals — all numeric values render), hero-amount-at-risk with three metrics, role-switcher lists 5 roles, role gating works (btn-request-budget hidden for CTO, visible for Project Lead on both Dashboard and Projects), full RequestBudgetDialog flow works (all 9 testids present, empty submit shows error toast, filled submit shows success toast and closes dialog), all 7 charts render, projects page shows 8 cards with search+filters, project detail exposes 6 tabs, approvals page shows 4 rows with approve/reject, settings has currency toggle + 6 switches + 8 team rows + 3 exports. 0 'Budget Consolidation' occurrences anywhere in visible text. Body computed bg is rgb(8,8,12) — matches spec.

issues: Benign but noisy: 10+ warnings 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on Dashboard mount. Same as iteration 1 — set explicit minHeight on ResponsiveContainer parents to silence.

action_items: MINOR (React hydration warning): Opening the Request Budget dialog logs a console error 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>'. Source appears to be /app/frontend/src/components/RequestBudgetDialog.jsx line 67 native <select><option>{p.name} · {p.client}</option>. The bullet character (·) is fine; the warning likely stems from React reconciliation with the Tailwind arbitrary-value className on the <option>. Recommend simplifying the option to plain text (drop className) or migrating to the Radix Select component like Settings/Approvals use. Not blocking — dialog still submits successfully.
OPTIONAL (from iteration 1, still applicable): Recharts width(-1) warnings clutter the console; add minHeight to chart containers.
critical_code_review_comments: RequestBudgetDialog uses a raw <select> in an otherwise Radix-only surface — swap to <Select> for consistent dark styling and to remove the hydration warning.
Role gating logic is duplicated between Dashboard.jsx and Projects.jsx (both compute `const isPL = role === 'Project Lead'`). Consider a small helper/hook (e.g., useRole().isPL) to avoid drift.
KpiGrid renders 8 finance KPIs correctly, but Cash Runway shows '9 days · Critical' with mock data — confirm this is intentional demo styling and not a data issue.
No files exceed 700 lines. Env vars respected. No hardcoded URLs. Testids present on all interactive elements.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (14/14 review checks pass, 0 testids missing, 1 minor React warning)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None — all data mocked in /app/frontend/src/data/mockData.js. PORTFOLIO now includes cpi=1.04, spi, eac=$195.2k, burnRate=$5,000/day, cashRunwayDays=9, aiCostRatio=47%, pendingApprovalValue=$23k, pendingApprovals=4.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Base URL: https://budget-mgmt-preview.preview.emergentagent.com. All required testids from iteration 2 spec exist. Role gating flow: click role-switcher → role-option-project-lead → btn-request-budget appears on / and btn-request-budget-projects on /projects. RequestBudgetDialog has raw <select> which triggers a benign React hydration warning — flagged as action item. Default role is CTO; the CURRENT_USER (Vikram Kumar) label in sidebar still says 'CTO · Ethara.AI' even after switching role (Sidebar reads role from context, so it updates — verified). No 'Budget Consolidation' anywhere in DOM.
